summary: Frontend-only verification of Iteration 4 (responsiveness, real print, new Doctor board & Day sheet pages, paper bg refresh). 26 of 27 assertions passed. Background colour, sidebar/hamburger breakpoint behaviour, mobile drawer open+nav+close, new sidebar items (Doctor board, Day sheet), Doctor board (5 cards), Day sheet, real print windows for receipt/token/day-sheet (all contain 'Maple Hospital' and expected headings), outstanding-balance chips on Check-in, pies rendering inside cards at desktop, ComposedChart revenue tab all working. One real responsiveness bug found: Reports page overflows ~132px horizontally on a 414px viewport (the Today/Week/Month + date + Export CSV header strip does not wrap).

ui_bugs.issue: Mobile (414px) horizontal overflow of 132px. The hero strip with the Today/Week/Month segmented toggle + the long date label ('Reception desk · 2026-06-22') + the Export CSV button is laid out as a single non-wrapping flex row and pushes the page wider than the viewport. All other pages (Dashboard, Board, Day-sheet, Billing, Check-in) have 0px overflow at the same width.

design_issues.screen: Reports (all viewports) and Billing receipt popup
issues: Console emits repeated Recharts 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart' warnings on Reports — these come from charts measured while their tab/container is still 0×0 (inactive tabs and the hidden print iframe). Cosmetic; not blocking, but worth wrapping non-active tabs with conditional render or giving ResponsiveContainer a minWidth/minHeight.
Radix DialogContent warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby' on the Pay dialog in Billing — a11y nit, add SheetDescription/DialogDescription.

action_items: Fix Reports page mobile layout: wrap the Today/Week/Month + date + Export CSV header into a flex-wrap (or stack on <sm) so the page doesn't horizontally overflow on 414px viewport.
(Nice-to-have) Suppress Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on Reports — either render inactive tab content lazily or pass min dimensions to ResponsiveContainer.
(Nice-to-have) Add a DialogDescription to the Billing Pay dialog to silence Radix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: print.js uses window.open without a fallback; if a popup blocker silently denies (return value null) the click currently no-ops with no user feedback. Consider toast('Popup blocked — allow popups to print') when w is null.

Sidebar.jsx exports two components from one file (Sidebar + MobileSidebarTrigger). Fine for now, but the duplicated rendering means both desktop sidebar AND the mobile sheet contain nav links with the SAME data-testid (e.g. 'nav-dashboard') in the DOM at the same time on tablet/mobile (sidebar is display:none but still in DOM). Tests using strict locator selectors will hit ambiguity. Consider adding a wrapper-scoped testid or unique-prefixed testids per surface.
DoctorBoard / DaySheet pages are net new; recommend adding a unit/integration smoke that they at least mount without runtime errors as part of CI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 is essentially green: paper bg #EFF1EC body + #E5E9E1 sidebar verified via getComputedStyle. Hamburger appears strictly <lg (1024) and sidebar is display:none below that — exactly matches the spec. Mobile drawer opens, all nav items render, clicking any nav item navigates and closes the sheet. All three print windows (receipt, token, day-sheet) open as popups and contain the 'Maple Hospital' header plus their expected section titles. Outstanding-balance chip count on Check-in returned 1 visible (one chip rendered for the currently-listed appointment with balance>0 — others appear when their appointment shows in the list, gated by current-day filter). Only failure to retest after main-agent fix: /reception/reports horizontal overflow at 414px (currently 132px overflow). The fix is purely CSS (flex-wrap or stack-on-sm on the header strip).
rca_of_the_issue: Reports page header strip is a single horizontal flex row (segmented toggle + long date label + Export CSV button) without flex-wrap or responsive stacking, so at 414px viewport the total intrinsic width exceeds the viewport by ~132px causing a horizontal scrollbar. Fix: apply 'flex flex-wrap gap-2' (or 'flex-col sm:flex-row') to that container in /app/frontend/src/pages/Reports.jsx hero strip.
